message:
test(tmux_cc): property-based chunk-boundary invariance for Parser (ft-3j0s0)

Implement the regression coverage I filed as ft-3j0s0 in 092fca77 —
tmux_cc's Parser must produce the same event sequence regardless of
how the incoming script is sliced across advance_bytes / advance_string
calls. A single chunk boundary that falls inside a %begin/%end guard
block, inside a UTF-8 multi-byte sequence, or between a command and its
trailing newline must NOT change what the parser emits — any divergence
is a regression in the streaming state machine.

## Generators

  - arb_simple_line: emits one of the ~15 simple tmux_cc event lines
    (%sessions-changed, %pane-mode-changed, %continue, %pause,
    %window-*, %message, %paste-buffer-*, %config-error, %output,
    %extended-output, %layout-change, %session-changed, %exit,
    %subscription-changed). Parameterized where relevant (pane ids
    1..5, session ids 1..5, sample messages from a fixed pool).

  - arb_guarded_output_line: bodies for inside a %begin/%end block.
    Small fixed pool to keep the output consistent and parseable.

  - arb_script_block: oneof (ScriptBlock::Line, ScriptBlock::Guarded).
    Guarded blocks carry timestamp (1..1000), number (1..1000),
    flags (0..4), error bool, and 1..4 output lines. Terminator
    flips between %end and %error based on the error bool — both
    paths have their own state transitions in the parser.

  - arb_script: collection::vec(arb_script_block, 1..16) followed by
    push_script_block that formats each block into the wire syntax
    (with \n terminators on simple lines, %begin header + body +
    %end/error footer on guarded blocks).

## Test body

Inside proptest! with 128 cases:
  1. `expected` — parse the whole script at once via
     Parser::new().advance_string(&script).
  2. `actual` — parse the same script via parse_in_chunks(&script,
     &chunk_sizes) where chunk_sizes is a Vec<usize> drawn from
     0..16, length 0..64.
  3. prop_assert_eq!(actual, expected).

parse_in_chunks advances the parser chunk-by-chunk, clamping each
chunk size to at least 1 byte (Parser::advance_bytes panics on
empty slices historically) and joining any tail chunk on EOF so the
script is fully consumed. The chunk_size distribution 0..16 is
deliberately tight — it forces splits inside common token widths
(%pane=5, %sessions-changed=17, %begin header=~11 bytes) where
boundary bugs are most likely to appear.

## New hand-written unit test: newline gating

  parser_waits_for_newline_before_emitting_line_event

Minimal sanity check: advance_string("%sessions-changed") MUST
return Vec::<Event>::new() (parser holds the line, waiting for the
trailing \n). advance_string("\n") then MUST emit exactly
vec![Event::SessionsChanged]. This is the simplest instance of the
invariant the proptest exercises at scale — keeping it as a fast,
deterministic regression catch outside proptest.

Imports:
  - use proptest::prelude::*;
  - use super::* was already there.

Implements ft-3j0s0; bead closure will land in the next sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
